author: Niche Gamer
text:
PRAGMATA Review Some games entertain, some games impress, and then, on rare and exceptional occasions, some games truly matter. Pragmata is one of those rare games. After years of anticipation following its intriguing reveal trailer, Capcom’s ambitious sci-fi action game is finally here. It offers something that few titles in recent memory have achieved: a genuine emotional […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Castle in the Darkness II resurfaces with new trailer and Steam page Indie developer LABS Works has re-revealed Castle in the Darkness II, their long-awaited action-adventure sequel. Originally announced back in 2019, the new yet retro action-adventure game has a new Steam store page and a new trailer. The original game was released in 2015. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus the new trailer: Explore a land […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
SNK launches VS Studio led by Katsuhiro Harada SNK Corporation has announced the launch of VS Studio, a new internal development studio led by former Bandai Namco boss Katsuhiro Harada. The newly launched VS Studio is headquartered in Shinagawa, Tokyo and will work with SNK on their current and future titles. Harada left Bandai Namco at the end of last year after more than […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
86: Eighty-Six removed from streaming services while Blu-ray sells out The popular anime 86: Eighty-Six has been removed from both Crunchyroll and Netflix in multiple regions. If you go to the old Crunchyroll page or the Netflix page for the series, you’ll find that it has been quietly removed. We’ve reached out to both platforms for a comment, though this is likely due to the license […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Dark fantasy boomer shooter Nailcrown announced Publisher Bohemia Interactive and developer Destructive Creations have announced Nailcrown, a “savage boomer shooter” set in a dark fantasy world. Nailcrown is in development for Windows PC (via Steam), though a release date wasn’t announced. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its reveal trailer: Nailcrown is a savage boomer shooter packed with high-velocity gunplay, […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Zangyou turns Japanese office overtime into psychological horror Developed by Flicker Point Studio, newly revealed first-person psychological horror game Zangyou takes place inside a Tokyo skyscraper after hours. “Zangyou” refers to overtime work in Japanese, and the entire premise plays directly into that lonely late-night office atmosphere. You take control of someone trapped on the 32nd floor after an earthquake suddenly hits the […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Mixtape Review I’m not the biggest fan of narrative heavy games with little to no actual gameplay, but there was something captivating about Mixtape when it was first announced last summer. As a person who turned seventeen in the year 1999, I’m always baited by attempts to nostaglize my childhood, as if my generation deserves the chance […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Roguelite city builder Kingfish announced Indie developer FireVolt has announced Kingfish, a new co-op roguelite city builder game about managing and defending a kingdom on the back of a massive ancient beast. Kingfish is in development for Windows PC (via Steam) and unannounced consoles with a release set for sometime in 2027. “The King and Fish both have incomplete information, and […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
‘We are genuinely sorry’ – Outbound devs under fire after urging players to change negative reviews A lot of indie games build massive hype before release, but sometimes the launch itself completely changes the conversation around them. That seems to be exactly what is happening with Outbound, the cozy open-world van-life survival game from Square Glade Games that recently launched on Steam and consoles after gaining more than 1.5 million wishlists.  […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
MIRESI: Invisible Future showcases explosive combat in new trailer Developed by CONTROL9, a studio formed by developers who previously worked on Goddess of Victory: Nikke, and published by Smilegate, the upcoming RPG MIRESI: Invisible Future is scheduled for a worldwide release in 2026 across PC, Android, and iOS devices. The story follows a protagonist known as the Councilor, who travels across collapsing timelines inside […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6: Why Collectors Chase Rare Cars Forza Horizon 6 rare cars, rewards, and wheelspins give collectors more reasons to build a valuable garage and keep playing. For many players, Forza Horizon 6 is not only about finishing first. Winning races matters, but the bigger goal often becomes building a garage that feels valuable, personal, and worth showing off. That is where […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Armored Trooper Votoms: Die Graue Hexe gets release date in new trailer Bandai Namco Filmworks has announced a premiere date for Armored Trooper Votoms: Die Graue Hexe, the latest film in the Armored Trooper Votoms franchise. The new sequel film to the beloved mecha anime will roll out in two parts, with part one premiering on November 20 this year. Bandai Namco Filmworks also revealed new key visuals […]

author: Niche Gamer
text:
PATLABOR the Case Files reveals summer launch and new trailer Publisher Good Smile Company and developer Chime Corporation announced a release window for PATLABOR the Case Files, their new 3D action game based on the beloved mecha series. PATLABOR the Case Files is in development for Windows PC (via Steam) and PS5 with a release now set for this summer. A physical release is planned for PS5 in Japan in […]
